title: Experience
question: How much experience using Linux do you have?

options.text: Beginner: I have never used Linux before and need a user-friendly starting point.
Intermediate: I have some basic knowledge and am highly motivated to learn new things.
Pro: I am highly familiar with Linux systems and comfortable with advanced tasks.

title: Package Management
question: How complex can the installation of programs be?

options.text: Simple: I want an easy-to-use graphical software store, similar to a smartphone.
Neutral: Standard installation methods via package managers or basic terminal commands are fine.
Complex: I am completely comfortable building from source or using advanced terminal tools.

title: Control
question: Do you value a bulletproof or a flexible system?

options.text: Bulletproof: I need a highly stable system that rarely breaks, even if software is older.
Neutral: I want a good balance of system stability and having control over my software.
Flexibility: I want full control over the system, even if it requires manual maintenance.
No: I prefer a system that comes with many useful programs pre-installed out of the box.
Neutral: A standard selection of everyday software is perfectly fine for me.
Yes: I want a minimal base installation so I can build everything exactly as I want it.
Not important: Standard data usage and system telemetry are fine with me.
Neutral: I prefer balanced privacy settings without sacrificing too much convenience.
Important: Strict data protection and maximum privacy are absolutely essential to me.
No: I just want a reliable, standard system that gets the job done without surprises.
Neutral: A standard desktop experience without too many experimental features is fine.
Yes: I actively seek out unique, experimental, and cutting-edge features to play with.
Rare: I prefer rare, thoroughly tested updates with fewer changes to ensure maximum stability.
Neutral: A standard, balanced update schedule is perfectly acceptable for my workflow.
Regular: I want a rolling release model to get the absolute newest features and fixes immediately.

title: Keyboard Focus
question: Do you want to control your system via keyboard or mouse?

options.text: Mouse: I prefer navigating and controlling my system mostly with the mouse.
Neutral: I use a balanced mix of mouse clicks and basic keyboard shortcuts.
Keyboard: I prefer controlling everything via advanced keyboard shortcuts and terminal commands.

title: Modern Design
question: Is modern UI design important to you?

options.text: No: Pure performance and responsiveness are far more important to me than looks.
Neutral: I don't mind either way, as long as the system is functional and clean.
Yes: I highly value a polished, aesthetically pleasing, and highly modern look.
Simple: I want a system that is ready to use immediately without tweaking.
Neutral: I don't have a strong preference; basic customization is enough for me.
Many options: I want the freedom to deeply customize every visual and functional detail.
Not at all: I have absolutely no interest in gaming on this system.
Light gaming: I occasionally play retro games, indie titles, or less demanding games.
Triple A: I want to play the latest high-performance, resource-heavy games.
No: I prefer basic graphics, which is especially useful for older hardware.
Yes: I want high resolution, smooth visuals, and standard modern effects.
Enhanced: I need advanced graphical features like HDR, high refresh rates, and complex animations.
No: I am using weak or older hardware that needs a lightweight system (around 2GB RAM minimum).
Neutral: I have capable mid-range hardware that can handle a standard system (over 4GB RAM minimum).
Yes: I have powerful modern hardware with plenty of resources (over 8GB RAM).
No: I am using AMD or Intel graphics, so I don't need proprietary Nvidia drivers.
Yes: I have an older Nvidia card and need reliable legacy driver support.
Yes: I have a modern Nvidia RTX card and need the latest proprietary drivers to work smoothly.
